verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes HSP70-1, classically a cytoplasmic/nuclear chaperone with no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. However, there is substantial experimental evidence (flow cytometry on intact, non-permeabilized tumor and stressed cells, surface biotinylation) that HSP70 is displayed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane under stress, oncogenic transformation, and immunogenic cell death contexts. The surface-display mechanism involves lipid-raft association and possibly annexin/phosphatidylserine interactions; it is not a constitutive PM anchor. Multiple cancer immunotherapy programs (e.g., anti-HSP70 antibodies, NK cell-based therapies, ADCs) explicitly target surface HSP70 on tumor cells. Contextual buckets: cell_state_induced (YES — stress/transformation induces PM display); tissue_restricted not applicable; lysosomal_exocytosis potentially contributing; dual_localization possible but subordinate to cell-state induction; stable_surface_attachment possible via lipid/raft. Best-fit: cell_state_induced surface display on transformed/stressed cells.
